content.fact: All For One escapes Tartarus, turning the postwar crisis from a manhunt into a national collapse.
content.injection: After Tartarus, treat All For One as physically free and actively rebuilding pressure through escapees, not as a contained prison threat.

content.fact: Tartarus is not the only prison failure; the breakout wave destabilizes containment across Japan.
content.injection: Postwar Japan should feel under-secured: police and heroes face multiple escaped villains, not a single isolated Tartarus incident.

content.fact: The public no longer trusts hero society as a stable protective system after the war and prison breakouts.
content.injection: In Dark Hero scenes, civilians should be frightened, angry, and skeptical of hero authority; trust has to be earned locally rather than assumed.

content.fact: A visible number of heroes quit or withdraw as criticism, danger, and public resentment intensify.
content.injection: Do not portray the pro hero system as fully staffed and confident after the war; attrition and demoralization should shape response capacity.

content.fact: Deku leaves letters explaining One For All to his classmates, widening the circle of people who know his secret.

entries.title: Top Heroes Know More Than The Public

content.fact: Selected top heroes know enough about One For All and All For One to coordinate with Deku, while civilians remain uninformed.
content.injection: Keep OFA operational knowledge limited to Deku, All Might, selected top heroes, and direct allies; ordinary evacuees must not understand the full conflict.

content.fact: All Might follows and supports Deku with supplies and care, but he cannot physically keep pace with Deku’s escalating solo campaign.
content.injection: In Dark Hero scenes, All Might should be emotionally invested and logistically helpful, yet increasingly unable to stop Deku’s deterioration by himself.

content.fact: Endeavor, Hawks, Best Jeanist, All Might, and select police contacts operate as a narrow response network around Deku.
content.injection: Do not portray all pro heroes as fully coordinated around Deku; the active network is small, secretive, tired, and politically constrained.

content.fact: The Hero Public Safety Commission no longer functions as the clean command center it once appeared to be.
content.injection: After the war, avoid treating the Commission as an omnipotent bureaucracy; its legacy matters, but the crisis has outrun its control.

content.fact: Class 1-A is left behind with Deku’s letters and must process his absence while preparing for danger.
